2026-04-04T21:09:39.6913769Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6914626Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6915065Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6915433Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6915762Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6916002Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6916159Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6916337Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6916506Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6916738Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6916941Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6917237Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6917516Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m
2026-04-04T21:09:39.6917728Z [36;1m[0m

bug\\n\\nI have a hosted agent in Foundry. That hosted agent has some tools. When I try to get the list of tools associated with that hosted agent, I am not getting any tools back.\\n\\nHowever, this works with a prompt agent. I created a prompt agent and added web search tool to it. When I list the tools associated with the prompt agent, I get the tools list with web search tool in it.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nFor both hosted and prompt agents in Foundry, I should be able to get the list of tools attached/associated with that agent. Currently it only works with prompt agents.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nFor hosted agents, tools are not returned.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```\\nvar projectEndpoint = \\\"https://my-foundry.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/my-project\\\";\\nvar agentId = \\\"my-hosted-agent\\\"\\n\\nvar projectClient = new AIProjectClient(\\n\\t\\tendpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint),\\n\\t\\ttokenProvider: new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nvar agentRecordResult = await projectClient.Agents.GetAgentAsync(agentId);\\nvar response = agentRecordResult.GetRawResponse().Content.ToString();\\n```\\n\\nFor hosted agents, the response looks like this:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\\"object\\\": \\\"agent\\\",\\n  \\\"id\\\": Agent\\\",\\n      \\\"created_at\\\": 1771616539,\\n      \\\"definition\\\": {\\n        \\\"kind\\\": \\\"hosted\\\",\\n        \\\"container_protocol_versions\\\": [\\n          {\\n            \\\"protocol\\\": \\\"responses\\\",\\n            \\\"version\\\": \\\"v1\\\"\\n          }\\n        ],\\n        \\\"cpu\\\": \\\"1\\\",\\n        \\\"memory\\\": \\\"2Gi\\\",\\n        },\\n        \\\"image\\\": \\\"myacr.azurecr.io/my-hosted-agent:v4\\\"\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nFor prompt agent, the output of the same code looks like this:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n - MCPToolDefinition does not support the project_connection_id property like in Python MCPTool class\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nThe class MCPToolDefinition has no way to set the `project_connection_id` property like we can on the [MCPTool class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-ai-agents/azure.ai.agents.models.mcptool?view=azure-python-preview) in python.\\n\\nThe `project_connection_id` is a property that is necessary when creating an agent that uses a MCP tool already created in Foundry nextgen.\\n\\nHere are some examples in python:\\nhttps://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/main/sdk/ai/azure-ai-projects/samples/agents/tools/sample_agent_mcp_with_project_connection.py#L48\\n\\n```python\\n    tool = MCPTool(\\n        server_label=\\\"api-specs\\\",\\n        server_url=\\\"https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp\\\",\\n        require_approval=\\\"always\\\",\\n        project_connection_id=os.environ[\\\"MCP_PROJECT_CONNECTION_ID\\\"],\\n    )\\n```\\n\\nI believe the `project_connection_id` is metadata, in that it does not need to be in the MCPToolResource (i.e. per thread run) thus why it could be in the MCPToolDefinition.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nBe able to have extra dynamic parameters that can be passed to the REST API of Foundry to align with the Foundry nextgen platform.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nImpossible to set the the property in any shape or form.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```csharp\\nnew MCPToolDefinition(\\\"MCP-Server-Label\\\", \\\"MCP-Remote-URL\\\")\\n```\\n\\nMCPToolDefinition does not expose the `project_connection_id` property\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\nOS: Windows 11 25H2 The new SDK uses https://ai.azure.com/.default and this fails with the above message.\\nI can use Azure CLI Credentials and the application will work as it uses a different ClientID but our development and test environments current rely on DefaultAzureCredentials and Visual Studio with Azure Service Authentication.\\nThis all worked fine prior to the upgrade to the Azure.AI SDKs and the new structure. The root appears to be the VS App GUID not be registered with the Azure ML Services.\\n\\n1.1.0-beta.1 (2025-05-21) - Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent\\n1.0.0-beta.9 (2025-05-16) - Azure.AI.Projects\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nAs before - VisualStudioCredentials is able to authenticate against https://ai.azure.com/.default using VS App ID when using the latest version of
